name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: I'll have more to say soon but I want folks to know that even in the midst of this health crisis, I believe there's hope on the horizon for those who struggle with mental illness. But we in the Senate have to do the work to help make that vision a reality.   Time to get to work.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: I'm announcing I'm rolling out three bills this month addressing the mental health crisis in America. The theme that unites them all: access to resources.   When I struggled with depression, resources were there for me. Many Americans don't get those, let's change that.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: I'm grateful I got help, but I share this because I want anyone suffering from a mental health issue to know they are not alone. We can all help break the stigma around talking about this.  But also as a Senator, I recognize my unique position. And I want to use it.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Did I live happily ever after? Not quite. Mental illness isn't a box where you're healthy or not—it's a spectrum.   I struggled again in my 30s as a young mom. But after a few years of treatment, I improved and know I can turn to it if I need it again.  Happier IS possible.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: When my roommate first talked to me about how I hadn't been myself, I was in denial. But eventually, I realized it made sense to go see a counselor. I didn't know what to expect and honestly, I was embarrassed.   But the experience changed my life.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: When it started in college, I thought it was just a rough patch. But at some point, it got harder to cope with life's daily challenges.   Everybody who's wrestled with depression has their metaphor, but many can identify with the sensation of color draining out of the world.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: I want to share something personal for Mental Health Awareness month. We need more understanding and help for the millions of Americans who struggle with mental illness.  I'm not saying that just because I'm a Senator, I'm saying that as someone who has suffered from depression.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: I'll close by saying that this pandemic only exacerbated the childcare crisis. I've been working on this issue my entire time in the Senate—the way we do childcare in this country needs an overhaul.  So let's make sure childcare survives this crisis AND fix the system long-term.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: This isn't just the right thing to do, it's also an investment. Working moms contribute a huge amount to our workforce, if communities lack childcare, we are risking hamstringing our economic recovery.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: With more emergency funding, we can avert this crisis. We can allow providers to keep staff on payroll, get PPE, and get assistance for other expenses needed to make childcare work.  Our bill has 50 billion in funding for this effect.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: I'll touch on number two in this thread. Mass closures are a serious issue. Childcare has always had thin margins and an early survey indicated nearly half of all providers could not survive a two week shutdown.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Our plan has three pieces.    1) Providing support to providers caring for the children of those fighting this pandemic on the frontlines.  2) Preventing mass permanent closures in the childcare system. 3) Shoring up the system to make it work better in the future.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Also want to point out this crisis has been particularly hard on working moms. The @NYTimes had a feature on this recently, linked it here.   So what can we do to ensure we have a robust childcare system in place when we can return to normal?  https://t.co/2KwQvFKKuc

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Before I start, I'll just say this issue is personal. Both @SenWarren and I were young moms, our kids were always our first priority.   When you’re a working mom, you need to feel confident that your kids are safe and cared for.

name: Senator Tina Smith

tweet: Thanks for having me on @AprilDRyan.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Agreed. Bolton communicated with the President directly—if this trial is going to be fair, we need to hear what he had to say.  https://t.co/xQn3PYqBdd

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (5/5) Lawyers really doubled down on Hunter Biden & went so far as to say that Obama should have been impeached, using House standard. Honestly it was bizarre. Their close was more like a preview of political ads this fall—both negative & positive. The message was not too subtle.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (4/5) Makes it even more clear that we must have witnesses & documents to have fair trial. I don’t see how a member of the Senate could refuse to hear Bolton testify under oath now that we know something about what’s out there. This information will come out—why not hear it now?

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (3/5) It was also surreal to have no discussion at all of the big news this weekend, that Bolton in his manuscript apparently makes it clear that the President did want an investigation into the Bidens and the 2016 election in exchange for releasing the aid.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (2/5) Was surreal to see Kenneth Starr review history and precedent of impeachment. One of his points was that we have been in what he called an age of impeachment that we should leave behind. Not a lot of irony in his presentation, given his lead role in the Clinton impeachment.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (1/5) Today is Day #6. It’s been a second day of hearing from the President’s lawyers. A couple things stand out:

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (6/6) On the good side, the President’s lawyers were civil and measured, which I think will help us all listen with the “empathetic attentiveness” that the Senate Chaplin has encouraged.  It will be good to have a day or so to think on all this, and clear my head for next week.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (5/6) I’ve never been a member of a jury before—I’ve never even been called for jury duty. But it never dawned on me that my 1st trial would be in the U.S. Senate, & it would not include even this most basic element—witnesses, & ability for both sides to cross examine witnesses.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (4/6) If the President’s lawyers really believe the witnesses we’ve heard from so far don’t have all the facts about what happened here, why not hear from the witnesses, and see the documents, that could clear all this up? I don’t think this question is going to go away.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (3/6) What was interesting to me is how their presentation makes the case even stronger for why this trial needs witnesses and the documents.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (2/6) After three days of comprehensive, thorough review of the facts and evidence from the House Managers, the President’s lawyers tried to to poke holes in the House Managers’ arguments.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: (1/6) Day #5 was a short day, just two hours of presentation from the President’s lawyers. After the session I went up to the gallery with my colleagues Tammy Duckworth, Jeff Merkley, and Leader Chuck Schumer to do a quick press response to the proceedings.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Tomorrow the President’s counsel will take up their presentation. I’ll let you know my impressions. In the meantime, thinking of questions I want answers to, & I continue to hope we will hear from witnesses & see documents that will allow us to know the truth. Which is our right.

name: Senator Tina Smith

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: The House managers provided an excellent review of the constitutional rationale, history and precedent for impeachment for a President’s abuse of power. Even my colleagues with law degrees found it fascinating and full of good information.

name: Senator Tina Smith

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: Based on how things went yesterday, here’s Mitch McConnell's plan for how to hold an impeachment trial:      First, tell the prosecution that they can’t call any witnesses.  Not any. Tell them to argue their case and then let the jury (that’s us) ask questions.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: 3) McConnell's move to table seems designed to protect his members from a "no" vote on including documents and witnesses for this trial, but I bet Americans will see what's really happening here.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: The President's lawyers seemed to be speaking to an audience of one. On questions of whether the Senate should call for witnesses and documents, they talked about Mueller, Pelosi, and Schiff.  Sometimes it came off as a bit incoherent. They read from notes and seemed unprepared.

name: Senator Tina Smith
tweet: The House managers today relentlessly plowed through questions about witnesses and documents, and asked us essentially "Why WOULDN'T you want to know this? Why WOULDN'T you want to see the documents?"  I agree.

tweet: We got some beautiful snow in Washington today, which is getting me excited for MN this weekend. Starting tomorrow, I’ll be traveling from the Twin Cities up to Northern MN to meet w/ local leaders, workers, business owners, and families and talk about the issues they care about.
